text.tspan: WHAT A SERVICE MESH GIVES YOU
(language-agnostic, no app code change)

g.text: MESH vs CHASSIS u2014 who owns which concern?
COSTS / DOWNSIDES of the service mesh

text.tspan: SECURITY
u2022 automatic mTLS between services (encrypted in transit)
u2022 workload identity (SPIFFE)
u2022 fine-grained authorization (who can call what)
TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT
u2022 weighted routing (canary 1%/5%/25%/100%)
u2022 header-based routing (route 'user=beta' to v2)
u2022 mirror traffic to a shadow service
u2022 fault injection (chaos testing)
RESILIENCE
u2022 retries with backoff (per-route policy)
u2022 timeouts (per-route)
u2022 circuit breakers (outlier ejection)
u2022 connection pool limits
OBSERVABILITY
u2022 golden signals for EVERY service-to-service call
u2022 distributed traces (auto-stitched across mesh)
u2022 service-topology map (auto-discovered)
u2022 per-route latency histograms
MESH does well:
u2022 network-level: mTLS, retries, traffic-splitting, golden-signal metrics
u2022 cross-language: works the same for Python/Java/Go/Node
u2022 platform-team controlled: ops can change policy without redeploys
CHASSIS does well:
u2022 in-process concerns: business metrics, structured logging with context
u2022 ORM, async processing, config loading, validation
u2022 anything needing in-process domain knowledge
MOST MATURE ORGS: use BOTH u2014 lighter chassis when mesh handles network.
u2022 EVERY POD now has a sidecar u2014 +CPU, +memory, +startup time
u2022 OPERATIONAL COMPLEXITY u2014 control plane is a complex system itself
u2022 EXTRA NETWORK HOP u2014 every call goes through 2 proxies (u00b11ms latency)
u2022 DEBUGGING HARDER u2014 5xx could be in app, sidecar, or control plane
u2022 SKILL DEBT u2014 team must learn Envoy/Istio mental model
MITIGATIONS: 'ambient mesh' (Istio Ambient, Cilium) puts the data plane
at the node level instead of per-pod u2014 lower overhead, similar features.
